name: h2oGPT
tagline: Private local chat and document Q&A with open-source LLMs
seoTitle: h2oGPT: Private Local LLM Chat and Document Q&A
metaDescription: h2oGPT is an open-source, 100% private platform to chat with local LLMs and query your own documents, with a Gradio UI and OpenAI-compatible API.
overview: h2oGPT is an Apache-2.0 open-source project for chatting with private, local GPT-style language models and asking questions over your own files. It keeps an offline database of documents such as PDFs, Word and Excel files, images, video frames, YouTube links, audio, code, and Markdown, so nothing has to leave your machine.

It ships with a Gradio web UI and a command-line interface, both with streaming responses. You can run many model types, including LLaMa-2, Mistral, and Falcon, on GPU or CPU using backends like Hugging Face, llama.cpp, and GPT4All. h2oGPT also exposes an OpenAI-compatible proxy server, so it can act as a drop-in replacement for the OpenAI API.

On top of plain chat, it adds document Q&A with embeddings stored in Chroma, Weaviate, or in-memory FAISS, plus vision models, image generation, voice transcription, text-to-speech, web search, and code agents.
features: Private offline document Q&A over PDFs, Office files, images, video frames, YouTube, audio, code, and Markdown using a persistent Chroma, Weaviate, or FAISS database
Wide model support including LLaMa-2, Mistral, and Falcon, with GPU and CPU backends via Hugging Face, llama.cpp, and GPT4All
Gradio web UI or CLI with response streaming, document upload and viewing, and a bake-off mode to compare many models at once
Vision models, Stable Diffusion image generation, Whisper voice transcription, and text-to-speech with voice cloning
OpenAI-compatible proxy server for chat, completions, transcription, TTS, image generation, embeddings, and function tool calling
Web-search integration and agents for search, document Q&A, Python code, and CSV data

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to try h2oGPT is to create a fresh Python 3.10 environment and install the PyPI package, then launch a GGUF model. Docker is recommended for full capabilities on Linux, Windows, and Mac.

steps.heading: Set the PyTorch index for your hardware
steps.body: Point pip at the right PyTorch wheels. For CPU or Mac (M1/M2), use the CPU index.

steps.heading: Chat with a model
steps.body: Launch the Gradio UI with a GGUF model, then open your browser to start chatting.

useCases: Run a 100% private, offline ChatGPT-style assistant on your own hardware with no data leaving your machine
Build a searchable knowledge base by asking questions over your own PDFs, Office documents, images, and audio
Use h2oGPT as a drop-in OpenAI-compatible API so existing apps can call local models instead of a cloud provider
Compare many models side by side in the bake-off UI to pick the best one for a task
